location.office_time_required: up to one day per week from home (predominantly in-office)

level_signals: 8+ years of total relevant work experience required
Described as 'senior leader responsible for shaping and delivering large, complex, and high-impact strategic initiatives'
Base salary range $122,000–$261,000 depending on location, consistent with senior IC level
Ongoing engagement with senior stakeholders and executive sponsor

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree or the equivalent combination of education and relevant experience AND
8+ years of total relevant work experience
Lead end-to-end delivery of large, complex strategic programs, ensuring alignment to business objectives, regulatory considerations, and client outcomes within a financial services environment.
Shape program strategy by developing business cases, defining multi-year roadmaps, and recommending investment priorities informed by data, market trends, and competitive dynamics.
Develop and manage integrated program plans, including scope, timelines, budgets, and resources, while coordinating across multiple workstreams and functions to ensure seamless execution.
Establish and lead program governance, engaging senior stakeholders and executives to drive alignment, enable effective decision-making, and maintain transparency on progress and risks.
Proactively identify, assess, and mitigate risks, issues, and dependencies, demonstrating sound judgment and adaptability in complex, fast-paced, and ambiguous environments.
Monitor program performance, including financials, KPIs, and adoption metrics, and drive continuous improvement to ensure delivery of expected value and quality outcomes.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Preferred experience in a consultant and/or strategic planning role
Experience in Financial Services/Wealth Management industry a plus
Experience in managing successful relationships with senior leaders, cross-functional partners, and vendors
Strong project management and organizational skills; ability to successfully manage multiple projects simultaneously
Strong analytical skills and ability to think strategically and creatively
Proven ability to research, identify, interpret, and prioritize market needs
Experience in developing business cases and influencing change initiatives
Effective presentation and influencing skills, including strong written and verbal communication skills
Strong interpersonal skills - Works well in a collaborative, team-based environment
Demonstrated ability of successfully dealing with ambiguity and creating partnerships across business areas
Proven ability to navigate across organizations and develop partnerships along with gaining buy-in for strategic proposals
Demonstrated ability to build consensus at all levels
Ability to work effectively with a variety of functional areas: Advisory, Sales, Operations, Marketing, Client Experience, Service and Shared Services

explicitly_excluded: FINRA licenses are not required and will not be supported for this role.

scope.surface: Individual Investor (II) Program and Change Management — managing strategic programs for a business unit overseeing $200B+ in client assets and 1M investors
scope.business_model: B2C / B2B asset management — retail and institutional investors
primary_metrics_inferred: Business growth of Individual Investor segment
KPIs and adoption metrics for strategic programs
Client asset and investor base growth
core_responsibilities: Lead end-to-end delivery of large, complex strategic programs
Shape program strategy by developing business cases and defining multi-year roadmaps
Develop and manage integrated program plans including scope, timelines, budgets, and resources
Establish and lead program governance with senior stakeholder engagement
Proactively identify, assess, and mitigate risks, issues, and dependencies
Monitor program performance including financials, KPIs, and adoption metrics
Strengthen organizational capabilities by promoting best practices and coaching teams

green_flags: Compensation ranges explicitly disclosed across multiple geographies ($122K–$261K base)
Clear business context: $200B AUM, 1M investors — meaningful scope
High-visibility role with executive sponsor engagement — career accelerant
Broad preferred qualifications list signals openness to adjacent backgrounds
FINRA license explicitly not required — lower barrier to entry
red_flags: Hybrid policy allows only one day per week from home — very limited remote flexibility despite 'remote workers' pay bracket listed
Role is predominantly in-office at Owings Mills, MD — less attractive to candidates outside the area
All preferred qualifications are listed as nice-to-haves, making it difficult to gauge what the hiring bar actually is
Visa sponsorship not addressed — unclear for international candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years required, exec-facing responsibilities, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not directly stated; inferred from responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and II segment context)
compensation.base_range_usd (lowest and highest bands used to represent full stated range)
ambiguities: Compensation table lists 'remote workers' as one of the Maryland/Colorado/Washington pay brackets ($122K–$209K), implying some remote eligibility, but the Work Flexibility section says 'up to one day per week from home' — treated as hybrid/primarily onsite here.
The JD URL references 'Owings-Mills-MD' but the body does not explicitly name the primary city — Owings Mills, MD inferred as primary office location from URL.

All preferred qualifications are listed under 'Preferred' but some (e.g., large-scale PM experience, senior stakeholder management) closely mirror must-have responsibilities described in the role summary — classified as soft per JD section labeling.
